design.stale_failure_mode: docker exec rc=1 + stderr (loud, not silent)

concrete_correctness_gaps.scenario: user changes HEXA_DOCKER_MEM=8g for heavier workload; cache hit reuses 4g container
concrete_correctness_gaps.consequence: OOM in container with no indication that the larger config was ignored

concrete_correctness_gaps.scenario: two scripts in different directories; first caches 'docker', second runs from synced repo where 'external' would have been correct
concrete_correctness_gaps.consequence: 5s of suboptimal routing per cwd transition

concrete_correctness_gaps.gap: _routing_cache_lookup at line 251 reads file but does not check file mtime against TTL atomically — TOCTOU
concrete_correctness_gaps.scenario: two concurrent hexa invocations within 5s window race to write cache; one reads while other writes
concrete_correctness_gaps.consequence: inconsistent routing, partial line read could parse incorrectly
concrete_correctness_gaps.mitigation_existing: subshell read-redirect makes single-block read; partial mid-write is unlikely on small file but possible

concrete_correctness_gaps.gap: cache file in /tmp shared across all sessions
concrete_correctness_gaps.scenario: headless launchd job sets HEXA_RESOLVER_FORCE_UP=1, writes cache; user shell with no env reads same file
concrete_correctness_gaps.consequence: env_hash differs → cache miss, OK actually; but wasted file read

session_evidence: no cache hits observed in 2026-04-26 session because HEXA_ROUTING_CACHE was unset (default off)

remediation_options.approach: extend env_hash to include HEXA_DOCKER_CPUS, HEXA_DOCKER_MEM, PWD
default cache ON (HEXA_ROUTING_CACHE=1) once correctness gaps closed

verdict: CORRECTNESS_GAPS severity=LOW; cache is opt-in and stale failures are loud; closing G6.1/G6.2 enables safe default-on
